problem_statement: Meal scheduling currently combines full-page week swipe press-and-hold drag and a move-or-duplicate decision modal after drop. That gesture stack is powerful but too easy to trigger accidentally on touch devices and too costly for a lightweight planning flow.
in_scope: Limit week swipe handling to the plan calendar frame instead of the full page
Make touch drag-drop move by default and remove the post-drop move-or-duplicate modal
Move duplicate into an explicit action inside meal detail while keeping Prev Next and This week controls
out_of_scope: New calendar interaction patterns outside the current week view
requirements: Dropping on the same day is a no-op
Touch drag hold delay increases to 300ms to reduce accidental drags
Duplicate remains available through explicit meal detail UI
acceptance_criteria: Dragging to a new day moves the meal immediately without a follow-up choice modal
Swiping outside the calendar frame no longer changes week
A user can still duplicate a meal from meal detail without using drag-drop
risks: Removing duplicate from the drop flow may hide the capability if the meal detail action is not prominent
implementation_notes: Update src/components/PlanSwipeContainer.tsx src/components/PlanScheduleDnd.tsx and src/components/PlanMealTile.tsx. Keep the drag handle pattern but shorten the instruction copy once duplication is no longer part of drop handling.
